meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to implement a high-leverage client workflow to overcome project stagnation using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start mining for 'problems.' By implementing a structured client discovery workflow, you shift from guessing to building validated solutions. Use Antigravity to map business logic and Claude Code to execute the MVP, ensuring your development efforts directly align with high-value, revenue-generating client requirements.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks scrolling through forums, searching for the 'perfect' SaaS idea, only to find yourself paralyzed by the sheer noise of the market. The reality is that your next million-dollar product isn't hidden in a secret list of startup ideas; it’s buried in the operational inefficiencies of a niche industry waiting for a specialized tool.

True founders don't hunt for ideas; they harvest problems. By formalizing a client discovery workflow, you bypass the guesswork and build exactly what users are already screaming to pay for. In this guide, we’ll move from abstract concepts to a deployed MVP, leveraging the raw speed of Antigravity and the intelligent orchestration of Claude Code.

steps.title: Define the Niche Problem Space
steps.description: Identify a target audience with high-friction manual workflows. Focus on industries still relying on Excel or legacy software.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a B2B SaaS strategist. Analyze the daily workflow of [Target Niche] and identify three specific, high-frequency tasks currently handled via manual spreadsheets that are ripe for automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research and list 5 pain points for [Target Niche] professionals.'

steps.title: Architect the Client Discovery Workflow
steps.description: Create a standardized questionnaire and intake process to validate the urgency of the problem identified.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a structured JSON schema for a client discovery interview. Include fields for urgency, current spending on workarounds, and key feature gaps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a discovery_schema.json file based on the identified pain points.'

steps.title: Develop the MVP Core Logic
steps.description: Build the foundational logic that solves the most painful part of the workflow using clean, maintainable code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Python-based core engine that automates the primary workflow bottleneck found in step 1. Focus on modularity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate core_logic.py and include unit tests for the primary automation function.'

steps.title: Implement Rapid Feedback Loop
steps.description: Set up a lightweight interface to present the solution to potential clients for immediate validation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a simple, high-conversion landing page structure and API endpoint that allows users to input their data and receive an automated workflow summary.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a FastAPI endpoint in main.py to handle user workflow inputs.'

steps.title: Iterative Deployment
steps.description: Deploy the initial version to a staging environment to allow for real-world testing and rapid iteration based on user input.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a Dockerfile and deployment script optimized for rapid iteration on a VPS, ensuring environment variables are securely handled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate Dockerfile and deployment.sh script for the current project.'

pro_tips: Always charge for the pilot. If a client won't pay $50 for a beta, they won't pay $500 for the production version.
Focus on the 'Time-to-Value' metric. If your tool doesn't save the client at least 2 hours a week, it is not a SaaS, it is a hobby.
Build the dashboard last. Founders obsess over UI; clients obsess over data results. Ship the engine first.
Use Claude Code to write tests before features. It forces you to define what 'done' looks like before you start coding.
Every feature must map to a specific workflow step in your discovery document. If it doesn't, kill it.
content.next_steps: Take your discovery schema into the field. Conduct five 15-minute interviews with professionals in your chosen niche, refine your core logic based on their feedback, and push your first version to a staging server.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop guessing and start building profitable software. Stop waiting for inspiration and start engineering solutions that pay the bills. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Create a standardized questionnaire and intake process to validate the urgency of the problem identified.
Build the foundational logic that solves the most painful part of the workflow using clean, maintainable code.
Set up a lightweight interface to present the solution to potential clients for immediate validation.
Deploy the initial version to a staging environment to allow for real-world testing and rapid iteration based on user input.
